title: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP)
summary: 9609 A Level — ERP systems, integration benefits, costs, and implementation challenges.
learningObjectives: Define ERP and its main modules.
Explain benefits of integrated information systems.
Analyse costs and risks of ERP implementation.
Evaluate ERP suitability for case organisations.

simpleExplanation.title: One system for the whole business
simpleExplanation.summary: ERP integrates finance, HR, inventory, production, and sales on a single database with real-time data — but implementation is expensive, needs training, and often requires process redesign.
steps: Single database links finance, ops, HR, sales.
Real-time stock levels, orders, and capacity visibility.
High licence, hardware, and training costs.
Success requires business process redesign and change management.

flashcards.back: Software integrating core business processes on one database.
Finance, HR/payroll, inventory, production planning, CRM.
Real-time data — fewer silos, better decisions (7.2.3).
Staff resist change; processes not redesigned to fit system.
Accurate live data essential for JIT scheduling (4.2.2).
Cloud ERP cheaper; may be overkill for very small firms.
SAP, Oracle, Microsoft Dynamics — case may name one.

sections.content: **ERP** (9.3.3) — evaluate **implementation**, not just features. Link to **7.2 change communication** and **7.1.5 centralisation** of data.

sections.question: Manufacturer uses separate spreadsheets for stock, finance, and production. Frequent stock-outs despite warehouse full. Would ERP help?
sections.solution:
**Diagnosis:** **Information silos** — production doesn't see **sales forecast**; finance doesn't see **WIP** — classic **horizontal communication failure** (7.2.3).

**ERP benefits:** **Single truth** for **inventory**, **MRP** auto-orders materials (9.3.5), **finance accruals** updated live.

**Costs/risks:** **$500k+ implementation**, **12-month disruption**, **staff retraining** — need **change manager**.

**Verdict:** **Yes if growth continues** — cheaper than lost sales; **pilot one site** first; **clean data migration** critical.
